_purpose: Ontology-driven adaptivity. No black-box ML. Every decision traceable to a rule + data.

rules.name: Input mode scaffold
rules.trigger: Student fails same LinguisticTarget twice in current input mode

condition.pseudo: attempts - successes >= 2 AND last 2 attempts were failures AND inputMode != 'choice'
condition.code: const h = targetHistory[targetId]; const recentFails = h.attempts - h.successes; return recentFails >= 2 && inputModeLadder.indexOf(h.lastInputMode) > 0;

rules.decision: Step DOWN one rung on inputModeLadder for the same LinguisticTarget

example.scenario: Student tries lt_verb_tener_imperative_negative via typing, fails twice
example.action: Next activity: same target, but input mode = choice (multiple choice options)
example.why: The student may know the form but can't produce it yet. Recognition before production.

rules.escalation: If student succeeds at easier mode, next encounter returns to original mode

rules.name: Game type rotation
rules.trigger: Student succeeds at recognition game types but fails at production game types for the same target

condition.pseudo: success in ANY of [pair, listening, fill] >= 1 AND success in ANY of [conversation, translation, builder] == 0 AND attempts in production types >= 2
condition.code: const h = targetHistory[targetId]; const recogOk = ['pair','listening','fill'].some(t => (h.successByGameType[t]||0) >= 1); const prodFail = ['conversation','translation','builder'].every(t => (h.successByGameType[t]||0) === 0); return recogOk && prodFail;

rules.decision: Insert a BRIDGING activity: one step above current highest success on the production ladder

selects.change: GameType → next step on production ladder above highest success

example.scenario: Student recognizes 'no tengas' in pair/fill games, but fails in conversation
example.action: Insert builder activity: reorder [no, tengas, miedo, del, silencio] — structured production without free recall
example.why: Bridge between recognition (I know the answer when I see it) and production (I can construct it). Builder is the missing rung.

rules.escalation: After builder success, retry the conversation activity

rules.name: Spiral prerequisite revisit
rules.trigger: Student fails a target whose prerequisite targets haven't been practiced recently

condition.pseudo: student fails targetX AND targetX.cefr > 'A1' AND any prerequisite target from lower CEFR level has lastAttempt > 10 encounters ago
condition.code: const obj = ontology.getObjectivesForTarget(targetId)[0]; const prereqs = obj.targets.filter(t => cefrRank(ontology.getTarget(t).cefr) < cefrRank(target.cefr)); return prereqs.some(pid => currentIndex - (targetHistory[pid]?.lastAttemptAt || 0) > 10);

rules.decision: Insert a brief review activity for the stale prerequisite before retrying the current target

example.scenario: Student at A2 fails lt_grammar_imperative_negative. Prerequisite lt_grammar_subjunctive_present_basic hasn't been touched in 15 encounters.
example.action: Insert quick fill activity: 'El subjuntivo de hablar, tú: ___' (answer: hables). Then retry the negative imperative.
example.why: Negative imperative = no + subjunctive present. If the subjunctive form is rusty, the imperative will fail. Fix the foundation before the roof.

prerequisiteMap._note: These links come from the ontology. Examples:

rules.escalation: If prerequisite review succeeds, immediately retry the original target. If prerequisite also fails, apply Rule 1 or 2 to the prerequisite.

rules.name: Self-correction promotion
rules.trigger: Student demonstrates strong mastery of a target via voice — promote to self-correction mode

condition.pseudo: successRate >= 0.85 AND voice successes >= 2 AND attempts >= 4
condition.code: const h = targetHistory[targetId]; const rate = h.successes / h.attempts; const voiceOk = (h.successByInputMode.voice || 0) >= 2; return h.attempts >= 4 && rate >= 0.85 && voiceOk;

rules.decision: Promote to inputMode: self_correction — learner evaluates and corrects own production instead of receiving external feedback

example.scenario: Student has 4 attempts on lt_verb_tener_imperative_negative, 85%+ success rate, 2+ voice successes
example.action: Next activity: same target, self_correction mode — student says 'no tengas miedo', then evaluates: '¿Lo dije bien?' and selects correct/incorrect
example.why: Self-monitoring is a higher-order skill. The student doesn't just produce — they judge their own production. This is the bridge to autonomous learner.

rules.escalation: If self-correction succeeds → flag 'self-monitoring achieved' on the target. If fails → return to standard voice mode (do NOT scaffold down to choice — the student already proved mastery, the issue is metacognitive, not linguistic).

walkthrough.storyContext: Yaguará tells the student: the forest at night has sounds that can frighten. But fear makes you deaf.

steps.prompt: Completa: ___ miedo del bosque. (no tener, tú)
Yaguará dice: ¡___ miedo! El bosque es tu amigo.
steps.ruleAction: 2 consecutive failures in typing → downgrade to choice

steps.prompt: ¡___ miedo! El bosque es tu amigo.
steps.note: Student recognizes the form when they see it
Production in context — the form now has emotional weight

steps.prompt: Cande escucha un ruido. Yaguará le dice: ¡No ___ miedo, Cande!
steps.note: Return to original input mode (typing). Student can now produce the form.

walkthrough.summary: 6 encounters. 2 failures → scaffold → bridge → context → return to production. Every step traceable. No guessing.

scalingNote: These 3 rules compose. Rule 3 (spiral revisit) can trigger Rule 1 (input scaffold) on the prerequisite. Rule 2 (game rotation) generates the bridging activity that Rule 1 then scaffolds. The ontology is the lookup table — rules don't need to know content, only relationships. Adding a new LinguisticTarget to the ontology automatically makes it available to all 3 rules. Adding a new rule requires only the same data schema. No retraining. No model updates. Just one more IF-THEN.
